medications: Naproxen 500 mg as needed for pelvic pain
health.mental_health: Managing chronic pain flares while juggling a long-distance relationship and relocation planning; periodic insomnia and stress spikes around travel and job-change decisions, but no formal diagnosis documented.
recent_visits: Primary care follow-up for blood pressure and lipid labs (January)
OB-GYN visit for endometriosis management and pelvic ultrasound review (February)
Telehealth nutrition consult for cholesterol and weight management (February)
Pharmacy blood pressure check after medication adjustment (March)

finance.debt: Auto loan with ~18 months remaining and a credit card balance that fluctuates (~$3k–$5k) due to frequent flights and relocation-related deposits; keeping finances separate from partner until plans are finalized.
investments: High-yield savings account for relocation fund
recent_transactions: Round-trip flight Houston–Charlotte (partner visit) booked for mid-month
Nonrefundable apartment application fees and background check for an out-of-state unit
Copay for OB-GYN visit and pelvic imaging
Payment to a moving company for a preliminary estimate/holding fee

relationships: Long-distance partner (Marcus Reed, based in Charlotte, NC) planning to cohabitate after relocation
Adult daughter (Leena) in the Houston area; supportive but cautious about the move
Younger sister (Priya) who helps with logistics and is skeptical of the timeline
Close friend/coworker (Denise) who covers shifts during travel
social.social_media_activity: Mostly private accounts; posts infrequent family photos and food/fitness updates, avoids tagging partner or sharing travel dates; uses WhatsApp and FaceTime daily for the relationship.
private_conversations: Negotiating relocation timeline and whether to move before securing a job transfer
Discussions about fertility/menopause concerns and intimacy impacted by endometriosis pain
Arguments about shared finances, lease terms, and what happens if the relationship ends after the move
Safety planning for travel and keeping her Houston address private from acquaintances

schedule.work_hours: Typically Mon–Fri 6:00am–3:30pm; rotates one Saturday per month for cycle counts and peak-season coverage; on-call for urgent carrier issues.
regular_appointments: Weekly home blood pressure checks logged Sunday evenings
Monthly medication refill pickup (usually first week of the month)
OB-GYN follow-up every 3–6 months depending on symptoms
Biweekly evening video call 'date night' with partner (Wednesdays)
upcoming_events: Weekend trip to Charlotte to tour apartments and meet partner's family (next month)
Quarterly warehouse safety audit and OSHA readiness walkthrough (in two weeks)
Annual physical and fasting labs scheduled before the next medication renewal (next month)
schedule.daily_routine: Up at 4:45am, quick low-sodium breakfast, early shift managing dock and staffing; afternoons for errands and meal prep; evenings split between stretching/walking for pain management and long video calls; travel planning typically done late at night.

professional.company: Regional third-party logistics (3PL) provider supporting petrochemical and consumer packaged goods clients near the Port of Houston
projects: Implementing a new WMS module for inventory accuracy and cycle counts
Reducing pick errors through retraining and revised scan compliance
Carrier performance scorecard and detention-cost reduction initiative
Cross-training leads to cover for planned relocation transition
professional.performance_issues: Travel for the long-distance relationship has led to a few last-minute schedule swaps and increased reliance on her lead; she is careful to keep relocation discussions quiet while she explores transfer options, but stress has made her more short-tempered during peak volume days.
workplace_relationships: Strong rapport with the day-shift lead (Denise) who informally mentors newer supervisors
Tension with a night-shift supervisor over overtime approvals and staffing
Good working relationship with HR, but she has not disclosed relocation plans broadly

hobbies: Cooking heart-healthy Indian and Tex-Mex fusion meals
True-crime podcasts during commutes and meal prep
Gardening on a small patio (herbs, peppers)
Weekend thrift shopping and home organization
habits: Limits sodium and tracks cholesterol-focused meals most weekdays
Coffee in the morning; avoids energy drinks due to blood pressure
Uses a heating pad and stretching routine during pelvic pain flares
Keeps a private relocation checklist and shared calendar with partner
Occasional late-night wine on high-stress weeks, especially after difficult calls about moving
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a two-bedroom apartment in west Houston; one room is partly packed with labeled boxes and documents for an out-of-state move, while maintaining the space for her daughter’s occasional overnight stays.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly Mediterranean-leaning with vegetarian lunches; reduces red meat and fried foods for cholesterol; avoids very spicy foods when reflux flares; tries to meal prep to stay consistent during travel weeks.
